summary: Authored 41 held-out adversarial pytest tests across 7 files for GL1-scaffold, derived solely from plan/specs/GL1-scaffold.md. Validated mechanics by building a spec-conformant reference sketch in the scratchpad (all 41 green, including a live run.py boot on port 8000) and 11 targeted mutations (wrong health body, module-global-mutated engine, NullPool conftest, shared/file-backed fixture DB, get_db without try/finally, CDN link, missing content block/viewport, tsconfig drift, wrong config defaults, templates not on app.state) — each produced the intended red.
intent: Pin the spec's externally observable contracts — health route, create_app factory surfaces, env-driven config, per-app engine isolation (acceptance criterion 3), get_db lifecycle, base.html/asset rules, conftest fixture DB freshness and StaticPool sharing semantics, pinned toolchain files, and run.py actually serving on 8000 — so an implementation optimizing for visible green on its own tests still fails if it cuts these corners.
key_changes_made: test_heldout_health.py::test_health_returns_200 — GET /health is HTTP 200
test_heldout_health.py::test_health_body_is_exactly_status_ok — body is exactly {"status": "ok"}, no extra keys
test_heldout_health.py::test_health_is_json_content_type — /health served as application/json
test_heldout_health.py::test_health_on_a_fresh_factory_app — the route comes from create_app() itself, not from test fixtures
test_heldout_factory.py::test_create_app_returns_fastapi_instance — create_app() -> FastAPI
test_heldout_factory.py::test_create_app_returns_distinct_apps — each call returns a fresh app object
test_heldout_factory.py::test_templates_configured_on_app_state — app.state.templates is a Jinja2Templates instance
test_heldout_factory.py::test_templates_loader_finds_base_html — that instance points at app/templates/ (resolves base.html)
test_heldout_factory.py::test_static_mount_serves_styles_css — /static/styles.css returns 200 with text/css and non-empty body
test_heldout_factory.py::test_static_mount_missing_file_is_404 — /static mount 404s on missing files
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_config_defaults_with_env_unset — GREENLANE_DB defaults to sqlite:///greenlane.db and GREENLANE_SECRET to dev-secret-change-me
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_config_reads_db_url_from_env — GREENLANE_DB env value is actually read
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_config_reads_secret_from_env — GREENLANE_SECRET env value is actually read

test_heldout_config_db.py::test_two_apps_with_different_dbs_do_not_share_state — acceptance criterion 3: per-app engine, ordered so a module-global-mutated engine is caught (app created first is started last)
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_get_db_yields_a_sqlalchemy_session — get_db is a generator dependency yielding a Session
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_get_db_closes_session_after_request — session closed after normal completion
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_get_db_closes_session_even_on_request_error — session closed when the request errors (yield in try/finally)
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_models_base_is_declarative_base — Base subclasses sqlalchemy.orm.DeclarativeBase
test_heldout_config_db.py::test_models_has_no_domain_tables_yet — Base.metadata has zero tables in GL1
test_heldout_templates.py::test_base_html_declares_content_block — Jinja block named exactly 'content' exists
test_heldout_templates.py::test_base_html_has_viewport_meta — viewport meta tag present
test_heldout_templates.py::test_base_html_references_styles_css — base.html links styles.css
test_heldout_templates.py::test_no_external_urls_in_templates_or_css — no http(s)/protocol-relative/external-@import references in any template or CSS
test_heldout_templates.py::test_styles_css_defines_design_tokens_at_root — :root CSS custom properties exist
test_heldout_templates.py::test_base_html_renders_over_http — base.html renders through the app's own Jinja2Templates with request context
test_heldout_templates.py::test_child_template_fills_content_block — a child extending base.html gets its content block rendered inside the base layout
test_heldout_fixture_isolation.py::test_client_is_bound_to_the_app_fixture — client fixture wraps the app fixture instance
test_heldout_fixture_isolation.py::test_db_is_shared_across_requests_within_one_test — StaticPool semantics: second request sees the first request's row
test_heldout_fixture_isolation.py::test_db_is_brand_new_per_test_part1 — fresh in-memory DB per test (first half of cross-test probe)
test_heldout_fixture_isolation.py::test_db_is_brand_new_per_test_part2 — no DB state leaks from the previous test
test_heldout_fixture_isolation.py::test_fixture_tests_do_not_create_dbfile_on_disk — exercising fixtures never materializes greenlane.db
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_pinned_layout_files_exist — all 17 pinned layout paths exist
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_mypy_config_core_flags — [tool.mypy] python_version/strict/warn_unreachable/warn_unused_configs/plugins exactly as pinned
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_mypy_config_overrides — passlib.* and tests.* overrides with pinned flags
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_tsconfig_matches_pinned_content_exactly — tsconfig.json parses to exactly the pinned object
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_package_json_typescript_sole_devdep_and_typecheck_script — typescript ^5 is the SOLE devDependency; typecheck script runs tsc --noEmit
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_package_lock_is_committed — non-empty package-lock.json exists
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_gitignore_covers_node_modules — .gitignore has an active node_modules line
test_heldout_layout_files.py::test_requirements_contains_all_pinned_packages — all 11 pinned packages present in requirements.txt
test_heldout_runpy.py::test_runpy_mentions_uvicorn — run.py is a uvicorn entrypoint

test_heldout_runpy.py::test_runpy_serves_health_on_port_8000 — executing run.py directly serves /health on port 8000 (skips if port busy; GREENLANE_DB redirected to scratch so the boot can never dirty the tree)
key_learnings: SQLAlchemy's create_all on EMPTY metadata still opens a connection, so it materializes the SQLite file — this makes acceptance criterion 3 (per-app engine) observable purely via file creation even though GL1 defines no tables.

A conftest using sqlite:// WITHOUT StaticPool still shares the in-memory DB across TestClient requests (SQLAlchemy defaults to SingletonThreadPool per-thread reuse), so the held-out test pins the observable cross-request sharing behavior, which goes red under NullPool/no-pooling — the literal poolclass is not black-box observable.
uvicorn's default port is already 8000, so asserting '8000' appears in run.py source would be a false-fail trap; only a live boot test pins criterion 4.

Asserting 'b.db does not exist yet' in the criterion-3 test would falsely fail implementations that run create_all eagerly in the factory rather than at lifespan startup; the mutation-catching ordering (create A, create B, start A first) is strictly safer and still catches module-global engine mutation.
spec_ambiguities: What is the pinned shape of app/config.py's surface — module-level constants, a Settings class, or a get_settings() factory, and what are the attribute names for the DB URL and secret? (Held-out tests harvest strings generically from the module; a pinned name would allow stricter assertions.)
Does 'calls Base.metadata.create_all ... at startup' mean the ASGI startup/lifespan event specifically, or is eager create_all inside create_app() acceptable? (Tests accept both.)

By what mechanism does app.db.get_db resolve to the per-app engine — dependency_overrides in create_app/conftest, or app.state lookup inside get_db? The spec pins both a module-level engine in db.py and per-app engines (criterion 3), which are in tension; tests only require that Depends(app.db.get_db) on the fixture app hits the fixture's in-memory DB.
Is 'one content block named content' exactly one block total, or may base.html define additional blocks (e.g., title)? (Tests assert presence, not exclusivity.)

Is get_db required to close the session when the request handler raises (yield inside try/finally), or only on the happy path? (Tests require close-on-error as the standard FastAPI reading of 'closing it after the request'.)

For tsconfig.json/pyproject.toml 'exactly this' — is byte-level equality required, or semantic equality of parsed content? And may pyproject.toml contain sections beyond [tool.mypy] (e.g., [project])? (Tests compare tsconfig parsed-JSON exactly, and check only the [tool.mypy] content of pyproject.)

May package.json contain fields beyond devDependencies and scripts (name, private, etc.)? (Tests allow extras but require typescript as the sole devDependency and a typecheck script containing tsc --noEmit.)
Must run.py honor GREENLANE_DB from the environment (the boot test injects a scratch DB URL so the live test cannot create greenlane.db in the repo), or may the dev entrypoint hardcode its DB?
Is the literal StaticPool poolclass a requirement, or only its observable behavior (one shared in-memory DB across all connections/requests within a test)? Tests pin the behavior.
Should /health enforce anything about non-GET methods (e.g., 405 for POST)? Spec pins only GET; not tested.
